title: NOC 90010 – Manufacturing Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Manufacturing managers under NOC 90010 are the high-level decision-makers driving Canada's industrial output. In a country where the manufacturing sector contributes significantly to the GDP, these TEER 0 professionals are vital for optimizing production lines in industries ranging from automotive to aerospace. For immigration, this managerial role is highly prioritized in Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker) and specialized STEM/Industrial Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) draws, offering a premium pathway to Permanent Residency (PR).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Level0Major Group90 – Management occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesSub-Major Group900 – Management occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesMinor Group9001 – Managers in manufacturing and utilitiesNOC Code90010

duties: Plan, organize, and evaluate the operations of a manufacturing establishment or production department.
Develop and implement plans to efficiently use materials, labor, and equipment to meet production targets.
Plan and manage the establishment of departmental budgets.
Direct quality control inspection systems and develop production reporting procedures.
Hire, supervise, and oversee the training of employees in new equipment or production techniques.
employment_requirements: A bachelor's degree or college diploma in engineering or business administration is usually required.
Five to ten years of supervisory experience in manufacturing are mandatory.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate to Favourable. While some traditional sectors face consolidation, the rise of specialized manufacturing (EV batteries, medical devices) is creating a new wave of management opportunities.
labour_market_demand: Canada is currently reinvesting in domestic manufacturing to strengthen supply chains. There is a "Good" job outlook for manufacturing managers, particularly in Ontario’s automotive heartland and Quebec's aerospace hub. With the shift toward "Industry 4.0," managers with experience in automation and Lean Manufacturing are in extremely high demand.
Industrial Innovation: Lead facilities utilizing advanced robotics and AI-driven production.
High Executive Pay: Manufacturing managers are among the top-tier earners in the industrial sector.
Economic Stability: Work in a sector supported by massive government incentives for green manufacturing.
pathways: Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker): High priority for TEER 0 managerial roles.
OINP Employer Job Offer: Targeting managers in Ontario’s manufacturing zones.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Direct pathway for managers with established manufacturing offers.
